pull.title: test: Allow to set height in create_block
pull.body:
The `create_block` helper is often called with `create_coinbase`: `create_block(prev_hash, create_coinbase(new_height))`

This is fine, but a bit verbose and tedious to type each time. Also, it requires an additional import.

Improve this by allowing to set `height` in `create_block` directly, similar to other options, such as `ntime`, or `hashprev`.

verification.payload:
tree 9d8f25ee0ec90a54651b149132a9d1f6151ecbbb
parent 53f4743c2150d0ff10a4f7781e489131b8e030f3
author MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz> 1776265169 +0200
committer MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz> 1776266904 +0200

test: Allow to set height in create_block

Previously, it was only possible to set the height indirectly by calling
create_coinbase outside the create_block function. This is fine, but
verbose and not needed.

Just like hashprev can be set directly (instead of using the value from
tmpl), allow height to be set directly.

Also, use it in one place. The other places are done in a scripted-diff.

verification.payload:
tree 1156d694bf3337dcc0a207f6d710c984cf9ee8c2
parent eeee853631db95927c2f1c6ec9cf39e43917681f
author MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz> 1776265592 +0200
committer MarcoFalke <*~=`'#}+{/-|&$^_@721217.xyz> 1776267206 +0200

scripted-diff: Use new create_block height option

-BEGIN VERIFY SCRIPT-

 # Replace single-arg create_coinbase calls ...
 # ... followed by ntime arg
 sed --in-place --regexp-extended 's/create_block\((.+), create_coinbase\(([^,]+)\), /create_block(\1, height=\2, ntime=/g' $( git grep -l 'create_block(' )
 # ... not followed by any other args
 sed --in-place --regexp-extended 's/create_block\((.+), create_coinbase\(([^,]+)\)\)/create_block(\1, height=\2)/g'        $( git grep -l 'create_block(' )

-END VERIFY SCRIPT-

events.message:
scripted-diff: Use new create_block height option

-BEGIN VERIFY SCRIPT-

 # Replace single-arg create_coinbase calls ...
 # ... followed by ntime arg
 sed --in-place --regexp-extended 's/create_block\((.+), create_coinbase\(([^,]+)\), /create_block(\1, height=\2, ntime=/g' $( git grep -l 'create_block(' )
 # ... not followed by any other args
 sed --in-place --regexp-extended 's/create_block\((.+), create_coinbase\(([^,]+)\)\)/create_block(\1, height=\2)/g'        $( git grep -l 'create_block(' )

-END VERIFY SCRIPT-

test: Require named arg for create_block ntime arg

The named arg is useful, so that the two integral args (possibly
integral literals) `height` and `ntime` are not confused.

events.body:
tested ACK fa374715f6db30e35e657f071c3c09f6badd100e

The positional vs named args are still a bit confusing here, but the situation is definitely better than before.
I left a few suggestions, happy to re-ACK if you decide to take them.

comments.body:
> [eeee853](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35089/changes/eeee853631db95927c2f1c6ec9cf39e43917681f) _test: Allow to set height in create_block_:

Can we add a simple `feature_framework_unit_tests.py` test here to exercise this new fallback behavior?
```python
class TestFrameworkBlockTools(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_create_block_prefers_explicit_height(self):
        block = create_block(
            hashprev=1,
            tmpl={"height": 100, "curtime": 2},
            height=200,
        )
        assert_equal(CScriptNum.decode(block.vtx[0].vin[0].scriptSig), 200)
```

---

Nit: this is safe because `genesis` isn't passed here so a height number won't ever be falsy, right?

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -419,7 +418,7 @@ def test_sequence(self):
         bump_txid = self.nodes[0].sendrawtransaction(orig_tx['tx'].serialize().hex())
         # Mine the pre-bump tx
         txs_to_add = [orig_tx['hex']] + [tx['hex'] for tx in more_tx]
-        block = create_block(int(self.nodes[0].getbestblockhash(), 16), create_coinbase(self.nodes[0].getblockcount()+1), txlist=txs_to_add)
+        block = create_block(int(self.nodes[0].getbestblockhash(), 16), height=self.nodes[0].getblockcount() + 1, txlist=txs_to_add)
comments.body:
> [eeee853](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35089/changes/eeee853631db95927c2f1c6ec9cf39e43917681f) _test: Allow to set height in create_block_:

A positional arg was changed to a named one here, for consistency we could specify `hashprev` as well:
```suggestion
        block = create_block(hashprev=int(self.nodes[0].getbestblockhash(), 16), height=self.nodes[0].getblockcount() + 1, txlist=txs_to_add)
```

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -35,15 +35,13 @@ def set_test_params(self):
     def build_chain(self, nblocks, prev_hash, prev_height, prev_median_time):
         blocks = []
         for _ in range(nblocks):
-            coinbase = create_coinbase(prev_height + 1)
-            block_time = prev_median_time + 1
-            block = create_block(int(prev_hash, 16), coinbase, block_time)
+            block = create_block(int(prev_hash, 16), height=prev_height + 1, ntime=prev_median_time + 1)
             block.solve()
 
             blocks.append(block)
             prev_hash = block.hash_hex
             prev_height += 1
-            prev_median_time = block_time
+            prev_median_time += 1
comments.body:
> [fa37471](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35089/changes/fa374715f6db30e35e657f071c3c09f6badd100e) _test: Require named arg for create_block ntime arg_:

I'm fine with this as well, but the previous `block_time = prev_median_time + 1` also made sense since it avoided duplicating the calculation of the `prev`

> [fa49662](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35089/changes/fa4966214b6795720941a575107cc3ee7ea760bc) _test: Remove unused create_coinbase imports_:

nit: since we're modifying these imports manually we might as well simplify them - in [other](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/20ae9b98eab20117344cf31f7cde39cadd70ca22/test/functional/feature_chain_tiebreaks.py#L7) [cases](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35089/changes/fa374715f6db30e35e657f071c3c09f6badd100e#diff-301e50453701ff497c3a78407fedacc519575e3b677b1d63231b803c54b6626dR13) a single `create_block` import is a one-liner.
